qa_pairs.question_text: Daniel, can you give us more color on the Cirrus Analytics integration plan? Specifically when do you expect Cirrus capabilities to be available natively inside the Stratus console, and have you sized the cross-sell opportunity for fiscal 2027?
qa_pairs.answer_text: Aaron, we are very enthusiastic about the Cirrus combination. Subject to closing the deal by the end of fiscal Q3, we expect native Cirrus query capabilities to be generally available inside the Stratus console by the end of fiscal Q4 2026. On cross-sell, our early modelling points to roughly 40 million dollars of incremental ARR from existing Stratus customers adopting Cirrus during fiscal 2027, but we will refresh that view once the deal closes and integration kicks off in earnest.

qa_pairs.question_text: Helena, the margin guide raise is encouraging. How much of that comes from operating leverage versus the hiring slowdown you flagged last quarter, and is the 19 percent target conservative given the Q2 print?
qa_pairs.answer_text: Marcia, of the roughly 100 basis points of incremental margin in our updated full-year view, we would attribute about 60 basis points to the hiring slowdown taking hold faster than we modelled and about 40 basis points to underlying gross-margin improvement in Stratus. We feel good about the 19 percent target but we are deliberately leaving room for additional R and D investment in Cirrus post-close, so we would not characterise the guide as deeply conservative.

qa_pairs.question_text: Daniel, your competitor announced a generative-AI assistant for their observability platform last month. What is Nimbus's roadmap on the AI-assist front, and when can we expect to see something in market?
qa_pairs.answer_text: Theo, we have been building the underlying capability for several quarters and we plan to launch our Stratus Copilot product into general availability by the end of fiscal Q4 2026. We will preview the product at our user conference in November. We are taking a deliberately enterprise-grade approach, with role-based access controls, audit logging, and data-residency guarantees baked in from day one, which is what enterprise CIOs are telling us they need before they will roll out AI assistants in production.

qa_pairs.question_text: On the international side, your last call called out softness in EMEA enterprise. Has that environment changed and what are you assuming for second-half international growth?
qa_pairs.answer_text: Sandra, EMEA improved sequentially in Q2. Net new ARR from EMEA enterprise was up 22 percent quarter over quarter, although still down approximately 5 percent year over year. We are not yet ready to call a full recovery in EMEA. Our second-half guidance assumes EMEA returns to mid-single-digit year-over-year growth by fiscal Q4 2026, but we have built some cushion into the consolidated forecast in case that recovery slips.

qa_pairs.question_text: Daniel, on the customer concentration question. Your top-ten customers contributed roughly 18 percent of ARR last fiscal year. Can you give us an updated view on where that stands today and where you would like it to be over the medium term?
qa_pairs.answer_text: Greg, top-ten customer concentration at the end of Q2 was approximately 16 percent of ARR, down from 18 percent at the end of fiscal 2025, which reflects the diversification we have been driving. We would like to see that number trend toward 14 percent over the next 18 months, but I want to be clear that we are not actively trying to limit growth at our largest accounts. The trend reflects faster growth in the long tail.

qa_pairs.question_text: Helena, can you give us any update on the share repurchase program? You authorised 500 million dollars at the start of fiscal 2026 and you bought back roughly 80 million in Q1. What is the pacing assumption for the rest of the year?
qa_pairs.answer_text: Yuki, we repurchased an additional 95 million dollars of stock in Q2, taking year-to-date buybacks to 175 million dollars. We expect to fully exhaust the 500 million dollar authorisation by the end of fiscal 2026. The Board will revisit the authorisation level at the start of fiscal 2027.

qa_pairs.question_text: Daniel, broader question on platform consolidation. How are you positioning Nimbus against the hyperscalers' native observability offerings, and are you seeing any of your enterprise customers re-platform off Stratus onto those native tools?
qa_pairs.answer_text: Robert, our win rate against the hyperscaler-native offerings remained above 75 percent in Q2 in competitive deals we tracked. The pattern we see is that enterprise customers want a single pane of glass across multi-cloud and on-premise estates, and hyperscaler-native tools by definition do not offer that. We have not lost a single seven-figure customer to a hyperscaler-native tool in the last twelve months. That said we monitor the competitive landscape closely.

qa_pairs.question_text: Quick housekeeping. Can you confirm the expected timing of your fiscal 2027 financial-framework update? And is there any change to the medium-term 25 percent free-cash-flow margin target?
qa_pairs.answer_text: Annette, we plan to host our fiscal 2027 analyst day in late February 2027 where we will provide a refreshed multi-year financial framework. The medium-term 25 percent free-cash-flow margin target remains our north star and we are not changing it today.

commitments.commitment_text: Close the Cirrus Analytics acquisition by the end of fiscal Q3 2026.
commitments.source_quote: we expect to close the previously announced Cirrus Analytics acquisition by the end of fiscal Q3 2026, which will materially expand our footprint in real-time log analytics.

commitments.commitment_text: Full-year fiscal 2026 non-GAAP operating margin will land at approximately 19 percent.
commitments.source_quote: we now expect full-year non-GAAP operating margin to land at approximately 19 percent, up from our prior guidance of 18 percent.

commitments.commitment_text: Deliver positive GAAP operating income for the full fiscal year 2026.
commitments.source_quote: We also expect to deliver positive GAAP operating income for the full fiscal year 2026, which would be a first for Nimbus.

commitments.commitment_text: Native Cirrus query capabilities will be generally available inside the Stratus console by the end of fiscal Q4 2026.
commitments.source_quote: we expect native Cirrus query capabilities to be generally available inside the Stratus console by the end of fiscal Q4 2026.

commitments.commitment_text: Launch Stratus Copilot into general availability by the end of fiscal Q4 2026.
commitments.source_quote: we plan to launch our Stratus Copilot product into general availability by the end of fiscal Q4 2026.

commitments.commitment_text: Fully exhaust the 500 million dollar share repurchase authorisation by the end of fiscal 2026.
commitments.source_quote: We expect to fully exhaust the 500 million dollar authorisation by the end of fiscal 2026.

commitments.commitment_text: Reduce top-ten customer concentration toward 14 percent of ARR over the next 18 months.
commitments.source_quote: We would like to see that number trend toward 14 percent over the next 18 months, but I want to be clear that we are not actively trying to limit growth at our largest accounts.
